diary: Woke up early to tend my rooftop herbs—the basil's finally thriving in this sunshine. Drove down to the lab with that Monday-morning coffee buzz, caught up on model optimization before the whole team ditched for the USA-Wales match at Belltown. I'm not usually a soccer person, but the energy was infectious, everyone packed around the bar at 11am on a Monday. Grabbed lunch at Pike Place after—the market was chaos, but I found some fresh rosemary I needed anyway. Afternoon back at the lab felt productive, then volunteered at the food bank in Shoreline before heading home. The evening light hit the garden perfectly while I watered everything. Ended the day with my vinyl collection, letting some old Bon Iver play while the city cooled down outside. Felt good to balance research and community, somehow.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drove in, but it made me realize how lucky I am to have flexibility. I almost skipped the soccer match—work felt urgent—but my coworker insisted, and honestly, it was the right call. I needed that break. The food bank volunteer slot was already committed, so I made sure to leave the lab on time rather than get sucked into another optimization problem. The rooftop garden's been my meditation lately, so prioritizing that evening ritual felt important. Mood-wise, I started scattered but the match and community time grounded me. By evening I was genuinely relaxed, not just tired.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
